reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_97]]
Speaker 3: How are these programs designed to keep pace with the latest industry trends and societal needs in the United States?

[[BLOCK:srt_98]]
Speaker 1: Why don’t you take this one?

[[BLOCK:srt_99]]
Speaker 1: Sure, no problem.

[[BLOCK:srt_100]]
Speaker 1: For example, we have an outstanding psychology program. It’s one of our fastest-growing, most popular, and most distinctive programs.

[[BLOCK:srt_101]]
Speaker 1: We’ve brought this well-established program to China, combining San Francisco’s professional strengths with the expertise of leading psychologists and professors in China who are at the forefront of the field.

[[BLOCK:srt_102]]
Speaker 1: In essence, it brings together two cultures and two intellectual traditions.

[[BLOCK:srt_103]]
Speaker 1: I believe that integration has made it an exceptional program. As for artificial intelligence, we’re located right at the heart of San Francisco’s AI revolution.

[[BLOCK:srt_104]]
Speaker 1: You could say we’re closely connected to the city’s growth. We’re surrounded by companies, and the surge of investment in San Francisco is happening all around us—virtually encircling Golden Gate University.

[[BLOCK:srt_105]]
Speaker 1: That puts us squarely at the center of the AI wave, which we see as a major focus of the university’s future development.

[[BLOCK:srt_106]]
Speaker 1: That’s one of the main reasons we’re so excited about launching our new degree program—the Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence.

[[BLOCK:srt_107]]
Speaker 1: We’re pleased to offer this degree in multiple formats, including online courses in English, online courses in Chinese, and in-person classes at our San Francisco campus.

[[BLOCK:srt_108]]
Speaker 1: So whether—

[[BLOCK:srt_109]]
Speaker 2: I don’t have much to add, except to say that many of our alumni are already working in these fields and related industries.
